content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

October 31, 1991

Tracey S. Buck-Walsh
DIEPENBROCK, WULFF, PLANT
& HANNEGAN
300 Capitol Mall, Suite 1700
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Your Request for Informal Advice
Our File No. I-91-427

Dear Ms. Buck-Walsh:

This letter confirms our telephone conversations on August 30
and September 4, 1991, wherein we discussed Government Code
Section 84305 and mass mailing. I informed you that an
officeholder may use his or her state capitol address on a mailer.
Section 84305 requires that a mass mailing must include the name,
street address and city of the candidate or committee. Please
note that in the case of a controlled committee, Section 84305(c)
requires the name of the person controlling the committee in
addition to the other requirements.

I also informed you that Section 84305 only pertains to a
mass mailing, as defined in Section 82041.5. Furthermore, there
is neither a statute in the Political Reform Act nor a regulation
which requires disclosure of a candidate or committee's name,
address and city if it is not a mass mailing.

I trust this answers your question.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Jill Stecher
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JS:aa

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

embedding.qa_text: This letter is from the California Fair Political Practices Commission, dated October 31, 1991. It is addressed to Tracey S. Buck-Walsh at DIEPENBROCK, WULFF, PLANT & HANNEGAN, located at 300 Capitol Mall, Suite 1700, Sacramento, California 95814. The letter responds to Ms. Buck-Walsh's request for informal advice regarding Government Code Section 84305 and mass mailing. The Commission confirms that an officeholder may use his or her state capitol address on a mailer. Section 84305 requires that a mass mailing must include the name, street address, and city of the candidate or committee. Additionally, in the case of a controlled committee, Section 84305(c) requires the name of the person controlling the committee in addition to the other requirements. The letter also states that Section 84305 only pertains to a mass mailing, as defined in Section 82041.5. There is neither a statute in the Political Reform Act nor a regulation which requires disclosure of a candidate or committee's name, address, and city if it is not a mass mailing. The letter concludes with the statement: "I trust this answers your question." Sincerely, Scott Hallabrin Acting General Counsel By: Jill Stecher Counsel, Legal Division
